source.note: Unit 6 grammar charts and answer-key examples from the Our World Level 4 Grammar Workbook (GWB pages 24-27). Planner LP pages 202-209 (pdf_offset 193, sections grammar-1 pp.9-12 and grammar-2 pp.15-16) and Student Book pages 98-101 also consulted.

grammarPoints.subject: english
grammarPoints.title: Obligation and prohibition: have to, must, can't, and don't
grammarPoints.rule: Use have to and must to talk about obligation (things that are important or required to do). Use can't and don't to talk about prohibition (things that are not OK to do).
grammarPoints.pattern: subject + have to / must + base verb  |  can't / Don't + base verb

chart.title: Have to, must, can't, and don't — TR: 6.4

workbookChart.label: Grammar Chart
workbookChart.title: Have to, must, can't, and don't

seeHowItWorks.explanation: Use have to and must to talk about obligation. Use can't and don't to talk about prohibition.

rows.example: We have to keep the oceans clean.
We must protect the oceans.
You can't throw trash into the ocean.
Don't leave food on the beach.

workbookChart.rule: Use have to and must for obligation. Use can't and don't for prohibition.
workbookChart.but: We have to keep the oceans clean. = We must keep the oceans clean. (same meaning; must is stronger/more formal)

intro_examples.text: We have to keep the oceans clean.
intro_examples.jp: 私たちは海をきれいに保たなければなりません。
私たちは海を守らなければなりません。

intro_examples.text: You can't throw trash into the ocean.
intro_examples.jp: 海にごみを捨ててはいけません。

intro_examples.text: Don't leave food on the beach.
intro_examples.jp: ビーチに食べ物を置いていかないでください。
私たちは自然の生息地を守らなければなりません。

intro_examples.text: Don't leave trash in the classroom.
intro_examples.jp: 教室にごみを置いていかないでください。

rows.example: We have to protect the whales and the dolphins.
rows.jp: have toは「〜しなければならない」という意味です。

rows.example: We must respect and protect nature.
rows.jp: mustはhave toより強い義務を表します。

rows.example: We can't throw bottles into the ocean.
rows.jp: can'tは「〜してはいけない」という禁止を表します。

rows.example: Don't leave garbage on the beach.
rows.jp: Don't〜は「〜しないでください」という命令形の禁止です。

rows.pattern: have to / must = obligation | can't / don't = prohibition
rows.example: Use have to or must for what is important to do. Use can't or don't for what is not OK to do.
rows.jp: 義務にはhave to/mustを、禁止にはcan't/don'tを使います。

chart.note_rule: Use have to and must to talk about obligation (things that are very important to do). Use can't and don't to talk about prohibition (things that are not OK to do).
chart.note_exception: must is stronger and more formal than have to; they often mean the same thing.
chart.note_exception_detail: Don't is the imperative form and is often seen on signs. Can't is used in sentences with a subject.

table.title: Obligation and prohibition

cells: We have to keep the oceans clean.
You can't throw trash into the ocean.
Don't leave food on the beach.

notes: have to and must both express obligation.
can't and don't (imperative) both express prohibition.

tab1_samples.text: We have to keep the oceans clean.
tab1_samples.jp: 私たちは海をきれいに保たなければなりません。

tab1_samples.text: We must protect the oceans.
tab1_samples.jp: 私たちは海を守らなければなりません。

tab1_samples.text: You can't throw trash into the ocean.
tab1_samples.jp: 海にごみを捨ててはいけません。

tab1_samples.text: Don't leave food on the beach.
tab1_samples.jp: ビーチに食べ物を置いていかないでください。

tab1_samples.text: We must protect natural habitats.
tab1_samples.jp: 私たちは自然の生息地を守らなければなりません。

tab1_samples.text: We have to protect the whales and the dolphins.
tab1_samples.jp: 私たちはクジラとイルカを守らなければなりません。

tab1_samples.text: We can't throw bottles into the ocean.
tab1_samples.jp: 海にびんを捨ててはいけません。

tab1_samples.text: Don't leave garbage on the beach.
tab1_samples.jp: ビーチにごみを置いていかないでください。

tab1_samples.text: We must respect and protect nature.
tab1_samples.jp: 私たちは自然を尊重して守らなければなりません。

tab1_samples.text: We must learn about our oceans and tell other people about them.
tab1_samples.jp: 私たちは海について学び、他の人に伝えなければなりません。

rules.title: have to and must — obligation
rules.subtitle: Use have to or must when something is very important or required.
rules.jp_subtitle: とても大切なことや、しなければならないことにはhave toかmustを使います。

transforms.from: It's important to protect the oceans.
transforms.to: We have to protect the oceans.

transforms.from: It's required to recycle our trash.

examples.text: Everyone has to pick up their trash.
examples.jp: みんながごみを拾わなければなりません。
私たちはびんをリサイクルしなければなりません。

rules.title: can't and Don't — prohibition
rules.subtitle: Use can't or Don't when something is not OK to do.
rules.jp_subtitle: してはいけないことにはcan'tかDon'tを使います。

transforms.from: It's not OK to throw trash in the street.
transforms.to: You can't throw trash in the street.

transforms.from: It's not OK to leave food on the beach.
transforms.to: Don't leave food on the beach.

examples.text: You can't use your phone in the movie theater.
examples.jp: 映画館では携帯電話を使ってはいけません。
海を汚染しないでください！

rules.title: must vs. have to
rules.subtitle: must is stronger and more formal. have to is more common in speaking.
rules.jp_subtitle: mustのほうが強くフォーマルです。have toは会話でよく使われます。

transforms.from: We have to keep the oceans clean.
transforms.to: We must keep the oceans clean.

transforms.from: You have to wear a bathing suit at the beach.
transforms.to: You must wear a bathing suit at the beach.

examples.text: You must not touch the sea turtles.
examples.jp: ウミガメに触れてはなりません。
人々は責任を持って魚を取らなければなりません。

rules.title: can't vs. Don't
rules.subtitle: Use can't with a subject in a sentence. Use Don't to give a direct instruction or rule.
rules.jp_subtitle: can'tは主語のある文に使います。Don'tは直接指示やルールに使います。

transforms.from: People can't litter on the beach.
You can't run in the classroom.

examples.text: Don't throw trash in the street.
examples.jp: 通りにごみを捨てないでください。

examples.text: We can't leave garbage in the classroom.
examples.jp: 教室にごみを残してはいけません。

mixed_samples.text: We have to protect the whales and the dolphins.
mixed_samples.jp: 私たちはクジラとイルカを守らなければなりません。

mixed_samples.text: We must learn about our oceans and tell other people about them.
mixed_samples.jp: 私たちは海について学び、他の人に伝えなければなりません。

mixed_samples.text: We can't throw bottles into the ocean.
mixed_samples.jp: 海にびんを捨ててはいけません。

mixed_samples.text: Don't leave garbage on the beach.
mixed_samples.jp: ビーチにごみを置いていかないでください。

mixed_samples.text: We must respect and protect nature.
mixed_samples.jp: 私たちは自然を尊重して守らなければなりません。

mixed_samples.text: We have to use biodegradable products.
mixed_samples.jp: 私たちは生分解性の製品を使わなければなりません。

mixed_samples.text: People can't fish in the river here.
mixed_samples.jp: ここでは川で魚を取ってはいけません。

mixed_samples.text: We have to recycle our trash.
mixed_samples.jp: 私たちはごみをリサイクルしなければなりません。

mixed_samples.text: Don't pollute the ocean!
mixed_samples.jp: 海を汚染しないでください！

mixed_samples.text: What do you have to do to protect the ocean?
mixed_samples.jp: 海を守るために何をしなければなりませんか。

explanation.title: Obligation
explanation.body: Picking up trash is something important to do — use have to or must.

tab3_quiz.jp: ごみを拾うのは義務なので、have toを使います。

explanation.title: Strong obligation
explanation.body: Recycling is very important — must expresses strong obligation.

tab3_quiz.jp: リサイクルはとても大切なことなので、mustを使います。

explanation.title: Prohibition
explanation.body: Fishing is not allowed here — must not expresses prohibition.

tab3_quiz.jp: ここでの釣りは禁止なので、must notを使います。

stem: use your phone in the movie theater.

explanation.title: Prohibition with can't
explanation.body: Using a phone in a theater is not allowed — can't shows prohibition.

tab3_quiz.jp: 映画館での携帯電話の使用は禁止なので、can'tを使います。

explanation.title: Don't for rules
explanation.body: Don't is used to give a direct rule or instruction without a subject.

tab3_quiz.jp: 主語なしの直接の指示にはDon'tを使います。

explanation.title: Obligation — important action
explanation.body: Protecting natural habitats is very important — must expresses this obligation.

tab3_quiz.jp: 自然の生息地を守ることはとても大切なので、mustを使います。

explanation.title: Prohibition
explanation.body: Throwing bottles in the ocean is not OK — can't shows prohibition.

tab3_quiz.jp: 海にびんを捨てることは禁止なので、can'tを使います。

stem: learn about our oceans and tell other people about them.

explanation.title: Obligation
explanation.body: Learning about the ocean is important — must shows this obligation.

tab3_quiz.jp: 海について学ぶことは大切なので、mustを使います。

explanation.title: Prohibition with can't
explanation.body: Bringing food to the library is not allowed — can't expresses this prohibition.

tab3_quiz.jp: 図書館に食べ物を持ち込むことは禁止なので、can'tを使います。

stem: protect the sea turtles — it's the law!

explanation.title: Strong obligation with must
explanation.body: A legal requirement is expressed with must — the strongest obligation word.

tab3_quiz.jp: 法律で定められた強い義務を表すのでmustを使います。

explanation.title: Prohibition — must not
explanation.body: Touching sea turtles is not allowed — must not is the formal prohibition form.

tab4_master.jp: ウミガメに触れることは禁止なので、must notを使います。

stem: choose his lunch at the restaurant.

explanation.title: Third-person have to
explanation.body: Has to is the third-person singular form of have to.

tab4_master.jp: 三人称単数にはhas toを使います。

tab4_master.cue: 私たちは海をきれいに保たなければなりません。
tab4_master.jp: 私たちは海をきれいに保たなければなりません。

tab4_master.cue: ビーチにごみを置いていかないでください。
tab4_master.jp: ビーチにごみを置いていかないでください。

explanation.title: Prohibition
explanation.body: Using lots of plastic bags is not OK — can't shows prohibition.

tab4_master.jp: プラスチック袋をたくさん使うことは禁止なので、can'tを使います。

tab4_master.cue: 私たちは自然を尊重して守らなければなりません。
tab4_master.jp: 私たちは自然を尊重して守らなければなりません。

explanation.title: Don't — imperative prohibition
explanation.body: Don't is used without a subject to give a direct rule.

tab4_master.jp: 主語なしの直接の規則にはDon'tを使います。

tab4_master.cue: 海にごみを捨ててはいけません。
tab4_master.jp: 海にごみを捨ててはいけません。

explanation.title: must not — formal prohibition
explanation.body: Fishing without a license is not allowed — must not is the formal prohibition form.

tab4_master.jp: ライセンスなしでの釣りは禁止なので、must notを使います。

tab4_master.cue: 私たちは責任を持って魚を取らなければなりません。
tab4_master.jp: 私たちは責任を持って魚を取らなければなりません。

japanese.title: 義務と禁止：have to、must、can't、don't
japanese.rule: have toとmustは「〜しなければならない」という義務を表します。can'tとdon'tは「〜してはいけない」という禁止を表します。
japanese.pattern: 主語 + have to / must + 動詞の原形 ／ can't / Don't + 動詞の原形

We must protect the oceans.
You can't throw trash into the ocean.
Don't leave food on the beach.
We must protect natural habitats.
We have to protect the whales and the dolphins.
We can't throw bottles into the ocean.
Don't leave garbage on the beach.
We must respect and protect nature.
We must learn about our oceans and tell other people about them.
Everyone has to pick up their trash.
We must recycle our bottles.
You can't use your phone in the movie theater.
Don't pollute the ocean!
People have to fish responsibly.
You must not touch the sea turtles.
We have to recycle our trash.
Don't throw trash in the street.
We can't leave garbage in the classroom.
What do you have to do to protect the ocean?

workbookActivities.instruction: Look and read. Circle the correct answer.

items.sentence: You ___ use your phone in the movie theater.

items: You must not touch the sea turtles.
We have to recycle our trash.

workbookActivities.instruction: Read and write. Use words from the box (can't, has to, must, must not).

sentences: We have to protect the sea creatures.
We must wear a bathing suit.
He has to choose his lunch.
They must not touch the shark.
I have to get up early for school.
I must go to bed before eleven o'clock.
I can't make breakfast by myself.

workbookActivities.instruction: Read and write. Answer the questions.

What do you have to do tomorrow?

items.question: What must you not do in school?
items.sample: I must not use my cell phone.

items.question: What can't you do before breakfast?

items.text: We must throw plastic bags in the ocean.
Don't leave trash in the classroom.
You have to throw trash in the rivers.
You can't use biodegradable things. They will never disappear.

We ___ learn about our oceans and tell other people about them.

lpActivities.instruction: Write. What about you? Write about rules at home and at school.
Compare your rules. Discuss in pairs.

source.note: Grammar chart and exercises sourced from Level 4 Grammar Workbook answer key, Unit 6 (pp.24-25); planner LP pp.202-205; Student Book pp.98-99.

grammarPoints.subject: english
grammarPoints.title: Future predictions with will and won't
grammarPoints.rule: Use will and won't to make predictions (guesses) about the future.

chart.title: Future with will and won't — TR: 6.7

workbookChart.label: Grammar Chart
workbookChart.title: Future with will and won't

seeHowItWorks.explanation: Use will to predict something that you think will happen in the future.

rows.example: Sea animals and plants will disappear.
We won't have as much food as we need.
Will people live longer?
Yes, they will. / No, they won't.
We'll live in crowded cities.
We won't live in small towns.

workbookChart.rule: Use will and won't to talk about the future. won't = will not.
workbookChart.but: Sea animals and plants will disappear. → We'll live ... (contraction with pronoun)

intro_examples.text: Sea animals and plants will disappear.
intro_examples.jp: 海の動植物は消えてしまうでしょう。

intro_examples.text: We won't have as much food as we need.
intro_examples.jp: 私たちは必要なだけの食料が手に入らなくなるでしょう。
魚と鳥は死んでしまうでしょう。

intro_examples.text: We won't be able to swim.
intro_examples.jp: 私たちは泳げなくなるでしょう。
酸素がなくなるでしょう。
私たちは健全な海を持てなくなるでしょう。

rows.example: Dolphins will swim near the top of the ocean.
rows.jp: willは未来のことが起こると予測するときに使います。

rows.example: Most creatures won't live in the ocean's middle layer.
rows.jp: won'tはwill notの短縮形で、未来に起こらないことを予測します。

rows.pattern: I'll / You'll / She'll / He'll / We'll / They'll + base verb
rows.example: We'll live in crowded cities. We won't live in small towns.
rows.jp: 代名詞とwillはよく短縮されます。

rows.example: Will people live longer?
rows.jp: willの疑問文はWill + 主語 + 動詞の原形の順になります。

rows.pattern: Yes, they will. / No, they won't.
rows.example: Will sea animals disappear? Maybe.
rows.jp: 短い答えにはYes, ... will. / No, ... won't. または Maybe.を使います。

chart.note_rule: Use will and won't to make predictions about the future. won't = will not.
chart.note_exception: When you are not 100% sure, you can use maybe: Maybe it will rain tomorrow.
chart.note_exception_detail: Will is the same for all persons: I/You/He/She/We/They will live in a different city.

table.title: Future with will and won't

cells: Oil spills will harm sea life.
Sunlight won't reach the bottom of the ocean.

notes: Use maybe when you are not 100% sure: Maybe it will rain tomorrow.
Contractions with pronouns: I'll, You'll, He'll, She'll, We'll, They'll

tab1_samples.text: Sea animals and plants will disappear.
tab1_samples.jp: 海の動植物は消えてしまうでしょう。

tab1_samples.text: We won't have as much food as we need.
tab1_samples.jp: 私たちは必要なだけの食料が手に入らなくなるでしょう。

tab1_samples.text: Dolphins will swim near the top of the ocean.
tab1_samples.jp: イルカは海の水面近くを泳ぐでしょう。

tab1_samples.text: Oil spills will harm sea life.
tab1_samples.jp: 石油流出事故は海の生き物を傷つけるでしょう。

tab1_samples.text: Most creatures won't live in the ocean's middle layer.
tab1_samples.jp: ほとんどの生き物は海の中間層には住まないでしょう。

tab1_samples.text: Sunlight won't reach the bottom of the ocean.
tab1_samples.jp: 太陽光は海底には届かないでしょう。

tab1_samples.text: Robots will help us with our work.
tab1_samples.jp: ロボットが私たちの仕事を助けてくれるでしょう。

tab1_samples.text: Tankers won't transport oil across the ocean.
tab1_samples.jp: タンカーは海を渡って石油を輸送しなくなるでしょう。

tab1_samples.text: There won't be trash in the sea in 100 years.
tab1_samples.jp: 100年後には海にごみがなくなるでしょう。

tab1_samples.text: People will take care of the oceans better than they do now.
tab1_samples.jp: 人々は今よりも上手に海の世話をするようになるでしょう。

rules.title: will — positive prediction
rules.subtitle: Use will + base verb to predict something you think will happen.
rules.jp_subtitle: 起こると思うことを予測するとき、will + 動詞の原形を使います。

transforms.from: I think oil spills harm sea life.
transforms.to: Oil spills will harm sea life.

transforms.from: I think people live in crowded cities.
transforms.to: People will live in crowded cities.

examples.text: Fish and birds will die if there is a big oil spill.
examples.jp: 大きな石油流出事故があれば、魚と鳥は死んでしまうでしょう。

examples.text: There will be no oxygen in the midnight zone.
examples.jp: 深海域には酸素がなくなるでしょう。

rules.title: won't — negative prediction
rules.subtitle: Use won't (= will not) + base verb to predict something you think will NOT happen.
rules.jp_subtitle: 起こらないと思うことを予測するとき、won't + 動詞の原形を使います。

transforms.from: I don't think we have healthy oceans.
I don't think there is any fish left.
transforms.to: There won't be any fish left.

examples.text: There won't be enough fish to reproduce.
examples.jp: 繁殖するための魚が十分にいなくなるでしょう。

examples.text: We won't have as much food as we need.
examples.jp: 私たちは必要なだけの食料が手に入らなくなるでしょう。

rules.title: Contractions with will
rules.subtitle: With pronouns, will is often shortened. won't = will not.
rules.jp_subtitle: 代名詞とwillはよく短縮されます。won't = will notです。

transforms.from: We will live in crowded cities.
They will not drive cars anymore.

examples.text: We'll know more about the ocean floor.
examples.jp: 私たちは海底についてもっとよく知るようになるでしょう。

examples.text: People won't use plastic bottles or plastic bags.
examples.jp: 人々はペットボトルやビニール袋を使わなくなるでしょう。

rules.title: Questions and short answers with will
rules.subtitle: Use Will + subject + verb to ask about the future. Answer with Yes/No + will/won't.
rules.jp_subtitle: Will + 主語 + 動詞で未来について質問します。答えはYes + will / No + won'tです。

transforms.from: People live longer in the future?
transforms.to: Will people live longer in the future?

examples.text: Will the children pick up the trash on the beach? Yes, they will.
examples.jp: 子どもたちはビーチのごみを拾いますか。はい、拾うでしょう。

examples.text: Will they travel in cars? No, they won't. They'll travel by bicycle.
examples.jp: 彼らは車で移動しますか。いいえ、しないでしょう。自転車で移動するでしょう。

mixed_samples.text: Fish and birds will die if there is a big oil spill.
mixed_samples.jp: 大きな石油流出事故があれば、魚と鳥は死んでしまうでしょう。

mixed_samples.text: We won't be able to swim if the beach is polluted.
mixed_samples.jp: ビーチが汚染されたら、私たちは泳げなくなるでしょう。

mixed_samples.text: Robots will help us with our work.
mixed_samples.jp: ロボットが私たちの仕事を助けてくれるでしょう。

mixed_samples.text: Tankers won't transport oil across the ocean.
mixed_samples.jp: タンカーは海を渡って石油を輸送しなくなるでしょう。

mixed_samples.text: People will take care of the oceans better than they do now.
mixed_samples.jp: 人々は今よりも上手に海の世話をするようになるでしょう。

mixed_samples.text: There won't be trash in the sea in 100 years.
mixed_samples.jp: 100年後には海にごみがなくなるでしょう。

mixed_samples.text: Will some types of fish disappear if overfishing continues?
mixed_samples.jp: 乱獲が続けば、一部の魚の種類は消えてしまうでしょうか。

mixed_samples.text: We'll know more about the ocean floor in the future.
mixed_samples.jp: 将来、私たちは海底についてもっとよく知るようになるでしょう。

mixed_samples.text: People won't use plastic bottles or plastic bags.
mixed_samples.jp: 人々はペットボトルやビニール袋を使わなくなるでしょう。

mixed_samples.text: The Green Team will clean up the river banks.
mixed_samples.jp: グリーンチームは川岸を掃除するでしょう。

explanation.title: will / won't + base verb
explanation.body: After will and won't, always use the base form of the verb.

tab3_quiz.jp: willとwon'tの後には動詞の原形を使います。

stem: affect the animals in the ocean.

explanation.title: Positive prediction
explanation.body: An oil spill harming the ocean is a negative event that will happen — use will.

tab3_quiz.jp: 石油流出事故が海の動物に影響を与えるという肯定の予測なので、willを使います。

explanation.title: Negative prediction
explanation.body: We don't expect people to live under the sea — use won't.

tab3_quiz.jp: 海の中で生活することはないという否定の予測なので、won'tを使います。

stem: people live longer in the future?

explanation.title: Will + subject for questions
explanation.body: For questions about future predictions, use Will at the start.

tab3_quiz.jp: 未来の予測についての疑問文はWillで始まります。

stem: Will they clean the beaches? No, they

explanation.title: Short answer — No, they won't.
explanation.body: A negative short answer uses won't.

tab3_quiz.jp: 否定の短い答えにはwon'tを使います。

stem: be trash in the sea in 100 years.

explanation.title: Negative future with there won't be
explanation.body: We predict there won't be trash — use won't.

tab3_quiz.jp: ごみがなくなるという否定の予測なので、won'tを使います。

stem: know more about the ocean floor.

explanation.title: Positive prediction
explanation.body: Knowing more about the ocean is something expected to happen — use will.

tab3_quiz.jp: 海底についてもっとよく知るという肯定の予測なので、willを使います。

explanation.title: maybe + will for uncertainty
explanation.body: When you are not 100% sure, use maybe + will.

tab3_quiz.jp: 確信がないときはmaybe + willを使います。

stem: have clean oceans if we work together.

explanation.title: Positive prediction with will
explanation.body: Working together to clean the ocean is something we predict will happen — use will.

tab3_quiz.jp: 協力すれば海がきれいになるという肯定の予測なので、willを使います。

stem: do many jobs that people do now.

explanation.title: Future prediction with will
explanation.body: We predict robots will take over many jobs in the future — use will.

tab3_quiz.jp: ロボットが多くの仕事をするようになるという未来の予測なので、willを使います。

explanation.title: Positive future prediction
explanation.body: Pollution harming the environment is predicted to happen — use will.

tab4_master.jp: 汚染が環境を傷つけるという肯定の予測なので、willを使います。

tab4_master.cue: イルカは海の水面近くを泳ぐでしょう。
tab4_master.jp: イルカは海の水面近くを泳ぐでしょう。

tab4_master.cue: 石油流出事故は海の生き物を傷つけるでしょう。
tab4_master.jp: 石油流出事故は海の生き物を傷つけるでしょう。

stem: Will they travel in cars? No, they

explanation.title: Short answer — won't
explanation.body: The negative short answer to a Will question is No, they won't.

tab4_master.jp: willの疑問文に対する否定の短い答えはNo, ... won'tです。

tab4_master.cue: 海にごみがなくなるでしょう。
tab4_master.jp: 海にごみがなくなるでしょう。

explanation.title: will + base verb
explanation.body: Use will + base verb for future predictions.

tab4_master.jp: 未来の予測にはwill + 動詞の原形を使います。

tab4_master.cue: 私たちは必要なだけの食料が手に入らなくなるでしょう。
tab4_master.jp: 私たちは必要なだけの食料が手に入らなくなるでしょう。

stem: people live longer in the future?

explanation.title: Will at the start of a question
explanation.body: For yes/no questions about the future, start with Will.

tab4_master.jp: 未来についてのyes/no疑問文はWillで始まります。

tab4_master.cue: 人々はペットボトルやビニール袋を使わなくなるでしょう。
tab4_master.jp: 人々はペットボトルやビニール袋を使わなくなるでしょう。

tab4_master.cue: 人々は今よりも上手に海の世話をするようになるでしょう。
tab4_master.jp: 人々は今よりも上手に海の世話をするようになるでしょう。

japanese.title: willとwon'tを使った未来の予測
japanese.rule: 未来のことを予測するときにwillとwon'tを使います。won't = will notです。
japanese.pattern: 主語 + will / won't + 動詞の原形

examples.sentence: Sea animals and plants will disappear.
We won't have as much food as we need.
Dolphins will swim near the top of the ocean.
Oil spills will harm sea life.
Most creatures won't live in the ocean's middle layer.
Sunlight won't reach the bottom of the ocean.
Robots will help us with our work.
Tankers won't transport oil across the ocean.
There won't be trash in the sea in 100 years.
People will take care of the oceans better than they do now.
We'll know more about the ocean floor.
People won't use plastic bottles or plastic bags.
Fish and birds will die if there is a big oil spill.
We won't be able to swim if the beach is polluted.
There will be no oxygen when pollution is severe.
We won't have healthy oceans.
Some types of fish will disappear if overfishing continues.
There won't be enough fish to reproduce.
The Green Team will clean up the river banks.
Will people live longer in the future?

workbookActivities.instruction: Read. Circle the letter of the sentence that describes the future.

options: a. We are overfishing, and it's hurting the ocean.
b. I won't pollute the ocean.
a. An oil spill will affect the animals in the ocean.
a. Will the children pick up the trash on the beach?
b. Do you always recycle your plastic trash?
a. I want to live under the sea.
b. People won't live under the sea.

workbookActivities.instruction: Unscramble. Look at pp. 94–95 of your Student's Book.

items.scrambled: top / will / near / the / of / the / dolphins / swim / ocean
items.answer: Dolphins will swim near the top of the ocean.

items.scrambled: oil spills / sea / harm / will / life
items.answer: Oil spills will harm sea life.

items.scrambled: ocean's / creatures / middle layer / the / most / live / won't / in
items.answer: Most creatures won't live in the ocean's middle layer.

items.scrambled: sunlight / won't / bottom / the / of / the / ocean / reach
items.answer: Sunlight won't reach the bottom of the ocean.

workbookActivities.instruction: Read and write. Complete the sentences with will or won't.

items.text: Robots ___ help us with our work.
Tankers ___ transport oil across the ocean.
There ___ be trash in the sea in 100 years.
We ___ use plastic bottles or plastic bags.
People ___ take care of the oceans better than they do now.

workbookActivities.instruction: Look at the Green Team's plan. Answer the questions.
greenTeamPlan: camp in the park near the river
invite people who live nearby to help

items.question: What will the Green Team do next?
items.answer: They will clean up the river banks.

items.question: Will they clean the beaches?
items.answer: No, they won't. They'll clean the river banks.

items.question: What will they write on their posters?
Who will help them?
items.answer: The people who live nearby will help them.

items.question: Will they travel in cars?
items.answer: No, they won't. They'll travel by bicycle.

items.question: Where will they camp?
items.answer: They'll camp in the park near the river.

workbookActivities.instruction: Write. What are four things you'll do to protect the planet? Use will and won't.
sampleAnswers: I will recycle my plastic bottles.
I won't leave garbage on the beach.
I will tell my friends about ocean pollution.
I won't use lots of plastic bags.

items.scenario: Oil pollution affects the sunlit zone of the ocean. What will happen if there is a big oil spill?
sampleAnswers: There will be oil on our beaches.
We won't be able to swim.

items.scenario: Pollution affects oxygen levels in the midnight zone and creates areas with no oxygen. Animals live in this area. What will happen to the animals?
sampleAnswers: When there is no oxygen, animals will die.

items.scenario: Imagine there are no more fish in the ocean. What will happen if there are no more fish?
sampleAnswers: There won't be any fish for animals and people to eat.

items.scenario: In some places, fishing boats catch too many fish. What will happen if overfishing happens for a long time?
sampleAnswers: There won't be enough fish to reproduce.
Some types of fish will disappear.

lpActivities.instruction: Play a game. Cut out seven pictures and the bingo card in the back of the book. Listen and play. Discuss.

source.note: Grammar chart and exercises sourced from Level 4 Grammar Workbook answer key, Unit 6 (pp.26-27); planner LP pp.208-209; Student Book p.101.
